LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING, WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. PETITIONER YU-TAI FU'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF ADMINSTRATIVE MANDAMUS PURSUANT TO CCP 1094.5 AND 1094.6 FOR AN ORDER TO CORRECT OR TO VACATE THE FINAL DECISION OF THE ACUPUNCTURE BOARD IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT IN LIGHT OF RULING ON THE DEMURRER. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. RESPONDENT ACUPUNCTURE BOARD'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS TIME-BARRED. (GOV. CODE 11523.) RESPONDENT ISSUED ITS DECISION ON NOVEMBER 15, 2012, AND IT BECAME EFFECTIVE ON DECEMBER 15, 2012. (PETITION, EXHIBIT A.) PETITIONER FILED THE INSTANT PETITION ON MARCH 11, 2013, WHICH IS UNTIMELY. CCP 1094.6 DOES NOT APPLY TO THE INSTANT ACTION BECAUSE THE DECISION WAS ISSUED BY A STATE AGENCY, AND NOT A LOCAL AGENCY. PETITIONER MAY NOT RELY ON HIS EXCUSE OR MISTAKE FOR RELIEF FROM THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. (KUPKA V. BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION (1981) 122 CAL.APP.3D 791, 794-95.) RESPONDENT WAIVED ANY DEFECTS IN NOTICE OF THE DEMURRER BY SUBSTANTIVELY OPPOSING THE DEMURRER AND FILING AN AMENDED OPPOSITION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: JUDGMENT FOR ORDER DENYING PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE (SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT)
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Emily Brinkman, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 415.703.5742, for respondent and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. RESPONDENT ACUPUNCTURE BOARD'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is Sustained without leave to amend. The petition for writ of mandate is time-barred. (Gov. Code 11523.) Respondent issued its decision on November 15, 2012, and it became effective on December 15, 2012. (Petition, Exhibit A.) Petitioner filed the instant petition on March 11, 2013, which is untimely. CCP 1094.6 does not apply to the instant action because the Decision was issued by a state agency, and not a local agency. Petitioner may not rely on his excuse or mistake for relief from the statute of limitations. (Kupka v. Board of Administration (1981) 122 Cal.App.3d 791, 794-95.) Respondent waived any defects in notice of the demurrer by substantively opposing the demurrer and filing an amended opposition. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
